decisions.matched_standard: Exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope
decisions.how: decider matched 'Exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope' for Walls; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Wingtip Monolith
alternatives.reason: The element is an exterior wall, matching the standard's intent for exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope. Although the element's room is null, the exterior context confirms applicability. The standard requires NFPA 285 compliance and prefers terracotta and fiber cement materials for reliability. Among preferred products, 'Wingtip Monolith' (fiber cement) is NFPA 285 compliant, preferred, and reasonably priced, fitting the lessons learned. Other preferred products are phenolic or metal panels, which are acceptable but fiber cement aligns well with the firm's reliable choices. Cost and wind load are project-specific, but the product meets the key fire and material preferences.

alternatives.name: Fabrikam Contour
alternatives.reason: Phenolic material, preferred and NFPA 285 compliant, slightly higher cost but reliable.

alternatives.name: Coho Kioto
alternatives.reason: Metal panel with tested wind load and preferred status, good alternative to fiber cement.

decisions.why: The element is an exterior wall, matching the standard's intent for exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope. Although the element's room is null, the exterior context confirms applicability. The standard requires NFPA 285 compliance and prefers terracotta and fiber cement materials for reliability. Among preferred products, 'Wingtip Monolith' (fiber cement) is NFPA 285 compliant, preferred, and reasonably priced, fitting the lessons learned. Other preferred products are phenolic or metal panels, which are acceptable but fiber cement aligns well with the firm's reliable choices. Cost and wind load are project-specific, but the product meets the key fire and material preferences.
honors_lessons: Terracotta and fiber cement have been our reliable choices

decisions.matched_standard: Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas
decisions.how: decider matched 'Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas' for Floors; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Initech Nordic
alternatives.reason: The element is a floor in a corridor, matching the standard's intent for resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas. The standard requires a minimum 20 mil wear layer for durability; fl-1322 has a 28 mil wear layer, exceeding this. It is also preferred by the firm and has Greenguard Gold emissions certification, aligning with lessons learned. Moisture limits are acceptable. This product balances durability, firm preference, and certification well.

alternatives.name: Coho Union
alternatives.reason: Firm preferred with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, slightly higher cost.

alternatives.name: Contoso Oakline
alternatives.reason: Firm preferred with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, lowest cost among preferred.

decisions.matched_standard: Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas
decisions.how: decider matched 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' for Ceilings; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Northwind Fissured 600
alternatives.reason: The element is a ceiling in an open office, matching the 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard intent and context. The candidate products all meet the category and fire class requirements. The open office is a direct match for this standard's intended room type. Among preferred products, cl-1022 has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, which aligns with lessons learned to avoid below 0.75 NRC and require humidity resistance near rooftop units. This product is preferred and meets all requirements, making it the best fit.

alternatives.name: Northwind Quietude 300
alternatives.reason: Preferred product with NRC 0.8 and standard humidity resistance, lower cost but less humidity resistance than primary.

alternatives.name: Proseware Vellum 300
alternatives.reason: Also preferred with NRC 0.8 and high humidity resistance, slightly lower cost.

decisions.why: The element is a ceiling in an open office, matching the 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard intent and context. This product is preferred and meets all requirements, making it the best fit.
honors_lessons: Avoid anything below 0.75 NRC in open offices
Require HumiGuard-equivalent humidity resistance near rooftop units

decisions.matched_standard: Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)
decisions.how: decider matched 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)' for Ceilings; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Litware Vellum 900
alternatives.reason: The element is a ceiling in a 'Pool Deck' room, which is a high-humidity wet environment. The 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms' standard explicitly targets such spaces and requires humidity resistance to prevent sagging. The 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard is for office environments and not suitable here. Among the high-humidity standard's products, cl-1124 (Litware Vellum 900) is preferred, fiberglass (preferred material for humidity resistance), has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, matching all requirements and lessons learned. This choice honors the lesson to avoid mineral fiber in humid rooms and prefer fiberglass or coated tiles rated for high humidity.

alternatives.name: Proseware Lyric 500
alternatives.reason: Preferred wood material with high NRC and humidity resistance, good alternative for wet environments.

alternatives.name: Litware Serene 300
alternatives.reason: Metal tile with very high NRC and humidity resistance, suitable for wet rooms but not preferred material.

decisions.why: The element is a ceiling in a 'Pool Deck' room, which is a high-humidity wet environment. The 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms' standard explicitly targets such spaces and requires humidity resistance to prevent sagging. The 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard is for office environments and not suitable here. Among the high-humidity standard's products, cl-1124 (Litware Vellum 900) is preferred, fiberglass (preferred material for humidity resistance), has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, matching all requirements and lessons learned. This choice honors the lesson to avoid mineral fiber in humid rooms and prefer fiberglass or coated tiles rated for high humidity.
honors_lessons: Prefer fiberglass or coated tiles rated for high humidity in wet rooms
Do not reuse open-office tiles in high-humidity rooms

decisions.matched_standard: Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)
decisions.how: decider matched 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)' for Ceilings; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Litware Vellum 900
alternatives.reason: The element is a ceiling in a locker room, which is a high-humidity wet room. The 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms' standard directly targets such spaces and requires humidity resistance to prevent sagging. The 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard is for office spaces and not appropriate here. Among the high-humidity standard's products, cl-1124 (Litware Vellum 900) is preferred, fiberglass (preferred material for humidity resistance), has high NRC (0.85), and meets all requirements. Mineral fiber products are discouraged due to sagging risk in humid rooms, so those are avoided despite some being preferred in the office standard. This choice honors the lesson to prefer fiberglass or coated tiles rated for high humidity and avoid open-office tiles in wet rooms.

alternatives.name: Proseware Lyric 500
alternatives.reason: Preferred wood material with high NRC (0.9) and humidity resistance, good alternative for wet rooms.

alternatives.name: Litware Serene 300
alternatives.reason: Metal tile with very high NRC (0.95) and humidity resistance, suitable for locker room ceilings.

decisions.matched_standard: Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas
decisions.how: decider matched 'Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas' for Floors; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Initech Nordic
alternatives.reason: The element is a floor in a corridor, matching the standard's intent for resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas. The standard requires a minimum 20 mil wear layer for durability; fl-1322 has a 28 mil wear layer, exceeding this. It is also preferred by the firm and has Greenguard Gold emissions certification, aligning with lessons learned. Moisture limits are acceptable. This product balances durability, firm preference, and certification well.

alternatives.name: Coho Union
alternatives.reason: Firm preferred with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, slightly higher cost.

alternatives.name: Contoso Oakline
alternatives.reason: Firm preferred with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, lowest cost among preferred.

decisions.why: The element is a floor in a corridor, matching the standard's intent for resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas. The standard requires a minimum 20 mil wear layer for durability; fl-1322 has a 28 mil wear layer, exceeding this. It is also preferred by the firm and has Greenguard Gold emissions certification, aligning with lessons learned. Moisture limits are acceptable. This product balances durability, firm preference, and certification well.
honors_lessons: 20 mil wear layer minimum in commercial corridors

decisions.matched_standard: Exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope
decisions.how: decider matched 'Exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope' for Walls; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Wingtip Monolith
alternatives.reason: The element is an exterior wall, matching the standard's intent for exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope. Although the element's room is null, the exterior context confirms applicability. The standard requires NFPA 285 compliance and prefers terracotta and fiber cement materials for reliability. Among preferred products, 'Wingtip Monolith' (fiber cement) is NFPA 285 compliant, preferred, and reasonably priced, fitting the lessons learned. Other preferred products are phenolic or metal panels, which are acceptable but fiber cement aligns well with the firm's reliable choices. Cost and wind load are project-specific, but the product meets the key fire and material preferences.

alternatives.name: Fabrikam Contour
alternatives.reason: Phenolic material, preferred and NFPA 285 compliant, slightly higher cost but reliable.

alternatives.name: Coho Kioto
alternatives.reason: Metal panel with tested wind load and preferred status, good alternative to fiber cement.

decisions.why: The element is an exterior wall, matching the standard's intent for exterior rainscreen cladding on the building envelope. Although the element's room is null, the exterior context confirms applicability. The standard requires NFPA 285 compliance and prefers terracotta and fiber cement materials for reliability. Among preferred products, 'Wingtip Monolith' (fiber cement) is NFPA 285 compliant, preferred, and reasonably priced, fitting the lessons learned. Other preferred products are phenolic or metal panels, which are acceptable but fiber cement aligns well with the firm's reliable choices. Cost and wind load are project-specific, but the product meets the key fire and material preferences.
honors_lessons: Terracotta and fiber cement have been our reliable choices

decisions.matched_standard: Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)
decisions.how: decider matched 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms (restrooms, locker/natatorium)' for Ceilings; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Litware Vellum 900
alternatives.reason: The element is a ceiling in a restroom, which is a high-humidity room. The 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms' standard specifically targets restrooms and requires humidity resistance to prevent sagging. The 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard is for open offices and not appropriate here. Among the high-humidity standard's products, cl-1124 (Litware Vellum 900) is preferred, fiberglass (preferred material for humidity resistance), has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, matching the lessons learned to avoid mineral fiber in humid rooms. This choice honors the lessons about humidity resistance and material preference for wet rooms.

alternatives.name: Proseware Lyric 500
alternatives.reason: Proseware Lyric 500 is preferred, has higher NRC (0.9), wood material suitable for humidity, also Class A fire rating.

alternatives.name: Litware Serene 300
alternatives.reason: Litware Serene 300 has highest NRC (0.95) and humidity resistance, though not preferred, good alternative for performance.

decisions.why: The element is a ceiling in a restroom, which is a high-humidity room. The 'Ceilings in high-humidity rooms' standard specifically targets restrooms and requires humidity resistance to prevent sagging. The 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard is for open offices and not appropriate here. Among the high-humidity standard's products, cl-1124 (Litware Vellum 900) is preferred, fiberglass (preferred material for humidity resistance), has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, matching the lessons learned to avoid mineral fiber in humid rooms. This choice honors the lessons about humidity resistance and material preference for wet rooms.
honors_lessons: Prefer fiberglass or coated tiles rated for high humidity in wet rooms
Do not reuse open-office tiles in restrooms

decisions.matched_standard: Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas
decisions.how: decider matched 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' for Ceilings; room fit 'match'

alternatives.name: Northwind Fissured 600
alternatives.reason: The element is a ceiling in an open office, matching the 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard intent and context. The candidate products all meet the category and fire class requirements. The open office is a direct match for this standard's intended room type. Among preferred products, cl-1022 has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, which aligns with lessons learned to avoid below 0.75 NRC and require humidity resistance near rooftop units. This product is preferred and meets all requirements, making it the best fit.

alternatives.name: Northwind Quietude 300
alternatives.reason: Preferred product with NRC 0.8 and standard humidity resistance, lower cost but less humidity resistance than primary.

alternatives.name: Proseware Vellum 300
alternatives.reason: Also preferred with NRC 0.8 and high humidity resistance, slightly lower cost.

decisions.why: The element is a ceiling in an open office, matching the 'Acoustic ceilings in open-plan office areas' standard intent and context. The candidate products all meet the category and fire class requirements. The open office is a direct match for this standard's intended room type. Among preferred products, cl-1022 has high NRC (0.85), Class A fire rating, and high humidity resistance, which aligns with lessons learned to avoid below 0.75 NRC and require humidity resistance near rooftop units. This product is preferred and meets all requirements, making it the best fit.
honors_lessons: Avoid anything below 0.75 NRC in open offices
Require HumiGuard-equivalent humidity resistance near rooftop units

decisions.matched_standard: Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas
decisions.how: decider matched 'Resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas' for Floors; room fit 'weak'

alternatives.name: Initech Nordic
alternatives.reason: The element is a floor in a Lobby, which is a circulation space but not strictly a corridor or high-traffic corridor. The standard applies to resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas, which fits the category and context well. Lobby is adjacent to corridors and shares circulation function, so room_fit is 'weak' rather than 'match'. The product list is all resilient flooring, meeting the category requirement. Among preferred products, fl-1322 'Initech Nordic' has the highest wear layer (28 mil) and is preferred, with Greenguard Gold certification, meeting emissions requirements. This product is selected for durability and firm preference. Lessons honored include minimum 20 mil wear layer and emissions certification. No lessons violated.

alternatives.name: Coho Union
alternatives.reason: Also preferred with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, slightly higher cost.

alternatives.name: Contoso Oakline
alternatives.reason: Preferred product with 20 mil wear layer and Greenguard Gold certification, lower cost but no higher wear layer.

decisions.why: The element is a floor in a Lobby, which is a circulation space but not strictly a corridor or high-traffic corridor. The standard applies to resilient flooring in corridors and high-traffic areas, which fits the category and context well. Lobby is adjacent to corridors and shares circulation function, so room_fit is 'weak' rather than 'match'. The product list is all resilient flooring, meeting the category requirement. Among preferred products, fl-1322 'Initech Nordic' has the highest wear layer (28 mil) and is preferred, with Greenguard Gold certification, meeting emissions requirements. This product is selected for durability and firm preference. Lessons honored include minimum 20 mil wear layer and emissions certification. No lessons violated.
honors_lessons: 20 mil wear layer minimum in commercial corridors
